narrative.summary: Specialist cheese + natural wine shop. Intimate, curated, serious about product. The kind of place where the person behind the counter knows every wheel. 4.8★ (277 reviews).

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Gerrard East where Leslieville starts to feel residential, and Good Cheese sits in a compact storefront that functions more like a European cave d'affinage than a shop. Inside, there's a refrigerated case running along one wall, wooden shelving stacked with natural wines, and just enough room for two people to stand at the counter while someone talks you through what's aging on the back racks. The lighting is bright enough to see the bloomy rinds properly, and the whole setup tells you this is about the product, not the Instagram.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is a buying experience, not a restaurant—wheels from small producers, the kind of stuff you won't find shrink-wrapped. Their Cheese Club boxes rotate through funk-forward picks with actual tasting notes that explain terroir and milk type, and they'll pack olives that convert people who claim to hate olives. The natural wine selection follows the same logic: low-intervention bottles chosen to pair with whatever stinky, washed-rind situation you're walking out with. Expect to spend $40-70 on a proper cheese haul, more if you're stocking up on bottles.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: You're planning a dinner party and refuse to show up with supermarket brie, or it's Saturday afternoon and you want someone who actually knows the difference between a Comté aged 18 months versus 24 to build you a board. Also solid for a weekend browse when you want to taste before committing.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "LOVE the Cheese Club box! Each cheese was unique and flavourful, with complex profiles I hadn’t tried before. I really appreciated the note sharing the background on each one - it made the experience even better. Such a great way to discover new cheeses. It arrived around 7pm the day of delivery, which was perfect.

And the olives were amazing (coming from someone who normally despises olives) and loved the surprise tote bag too."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I put in a fairly large order online. Got a call shortly after stating some cheese was now sold out but she recommended a great substitute and the I asked her if I was missing anything else and so she recommended more stuff. Next day was sent and following day I received it. I am 4 hours away and everything was fresh and well packaged. Superbe cheese, selection, service and packaging."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "✨️A Hidden Gem for Cheese & Wine Lovers✨️

Good Cheese is an absolute gem! From the moment I walked in, the two women working that evening made me feel so at home. They were warm, engaging, and incredibly knowledgeable about their selection. Their enthusiasm for cheese (and wine!) was contagious, making the experience even more enjoyable.

The store itself is beautifully laid out—thoughtfully arranged, inviting, and just a lovely space to browse. But what truly sets Good Cheese apart is that it’s not just a shop; it’s a place to slow down and savor. They don’t just sell wine—they serve it too, so you can sit back, relax, and enjoy a glass from their expertly curated selection.

If you’re a cheese lover, a wine enthusiast, or just someone who enjoys discovering special little spots, Good Cheese is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be back!"
